title: ScrapeResult
description: Output of wporg-scrap: one JSON file per WordPress.org plugin slug.

scraped_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the scrape started.

pub_date.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the post; may be empty if the source feed did not expose a parseable date.

body_md.description: Post body as Markdown, converted from the source HTML via turndown. May be empty for posts with no content.

topic_slug.description: Last path segment of the thread URL. Unique within a given plugin.

author.description: Username of the topic opener, taken from the listing row.

pub_date.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the opener's post, taken from the per-thread RSS feed (the listing row only exposes last_activity_at, not the original post date).

last_activity_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent post in the thread, taken from the listing row. May be empty if the source markup changes and the parser cannot extract it.

last_activity_author.description: Username of the author of the most recent post.

reply_count.description: Number of replies in the thread, not counting the opening post.

voice_count.description: Number of unique participants in the thread, including the opener.

is_resolved.description: True when the listing row carried the 'resolved' marker.

opener.description: The first post in the thread, identified as the item with the earliest pub_date in the per-thread RSS feed.

replies.description: All posts after the opener, in feed order. Empty array when the thread has zero replies.
